[210:26]What that is supposed to mean is that every year?

[210:30]The Senate can pass one appropriations bill

[210:34]That increases revenue, cuts spending, or affects the debt ceiling.

[210:40]It has to do one or all of those things.

[210:43]And you can invoke this once per year, it's called budget reconciliation.

[210:49]And the benefit is that you can pass it through the Senate with a simple majority.

[210:59]If you invoke budget reconciliation, which is a special process, you can do it once per

[211:04]year, technically up to three times, but historically just once.

[211:09]That's how it works.

[211:11]You can pass it with just a simple majority, just 51 votes.

[211:15]And that would allow the Republicans to pass a bill along party lines.

[211:21]Plus, JD Vance acts as a tiebreaker in the event of a tie vote.

[211:26]So they have 53 plus one, it would allow them to pass a bill with only Republicans.

[211:37]this budget reconciliation bill, they're calling it one big beautiful bill.
